ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
text: “Butɔ kɔ wɔnɔ, ɛshãdɔkasɛ anɛ Farisianɛ, wɔnɔ betroni! Nɔɔ kɔ wɔnɔ kɛdɔ huhuhu tirikpo nɛ kɛfɔ mɛ—aduve, dzakanɛ anɛ ɔsã. Pɔ nɔ shɛnahwɛ ɔshã anɛ ebui gɛganɛ nɛ mɛ—tɛnɛna, eshutukubui didi nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋa. Nɔ tɔ bhɛtɛ kidegba yɛ, klɛ nɔ gɛ tɔ shɛnahwɛ kɛku nɛ tsyɛ shu.

ref: You are nothing but snakes and the children of snakes! How can you escape going to hell?
text: “Wɔnɔ beblidzya! Wɔnɔ atagblashã ɛyamɛeyi! Kɛlɛ nɔ bɔ na sii lɔ kifumani manim zɛza kɛpɔ?

ref: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”
text: Gɛ Yesu ezhidzi Olivɛ butu nɛa bha, yɛ badɔkpasɛ nɛ aba yɛ ɔbha nɛ tɛkpukpum. Klɛ ba ɖɔ asɛ, “Tsre lɔ, ” “kibe ɖueɖu mɛ kibui nɛ ba ba kɛlɛ mɛ, klɛ kide gɛ ba dzi wɔ tɛbɛba nɛ kɛdɔmumɔ nɛ kibe nɛ tibibeke mɛ?”

ref: You will be arrested, punished, and even killed. Because of me, you will be hated by people of all nations.
text: “Ɔkɛlɛ la ba yɛkɔ wɔnɔ akpɛ ahwɛ kɔ sɛ ba kpatɔnɔ klɛ be be yi nɔ, klɛ amadi nɛ petee atɛ ba pɛɛ wɔnɔɛ bui akpɛ mɛ so.

ref: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
text: Alasɛ flogo tɛ kesukpa nɛa bha ka ba yɛkɛ buni, banɔa za ŋɛdɔ klɛ ba wɛ̃ dɔ, baa gbanɔ adzɛ klɛ baa yɛkɔ adzɛ akɔ sɛ ba gbanɔ, ɔdɔ nanɔ kibe lɛ gɛ Nowa etsoku otsyokpo nɛ mɛ;

ref: If that happens, the master will surely come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him.
text: Axwɛdɛ nɛ yɛ apalete nɛ aba ba nɛ kiwi nɔ nɛa bha gɛ etee di yɛ dzoenu klɛ nɛ ɔɖɔ nɔɔ nɛ mɛ gɛ etinyi.

ref: So, my disciples, always be ready! You don't know the day or the time when all this will happen. A Story about Three Servants (Luke
text: “Kɛlɛ so nɔ za nɛ awɔ̃, alasɛ no tinyi kiwi nɔɔ ɔdɔgɔsɛ ɔɖɔ nɛ mɛ. Kɛkpalɛ Denɔ Baxwɛdɛ Tɛata Shu

ref: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”
text: Kɛlɛ so isi wɔɛ fu klɛ mɛ ɛyɛkɔ ehoeyi nɛ gɛ ɔkɔm nɛ da tsu ozi pɛ ɛyɛkɔ kpɛ. Di, kɛ gɛ nu wɔɛyɛ nɛ nɛɛ.’

ref: Finally, Jesus returned to his disciples and said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
text: Ɔkɛlɛ etsodzi aba badɔkpasɛ nɛ ɔbha klɛ etsre balɛ asɛ, “Nɔ kodzi ɔdɔ tra pɛ no dzi ozo muene? No di, ɔɖa nɛ mɛ otsuɔba, klɛ ba ga Anɔdzyrame yɛ Eyi nɛesi akpɛ bagɛ ba bhɛtɛ buzizi nɛ ahwɛ.

ref: But when his disciples saw this, they became angry and complained, “Why such a waste?
text: Gɛ yɛ badɔkpasɛ nɛ amɔa na, ki kualɛ obulo. Be bisi asɛ, “Kide dɔ zizi kɛlɛ nui lɛlɛ?”

ref: When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, “One of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.”
text: Klɛ gɛ bedzi aŋa kɛdɔ nɛɛ, aɖɔ asɛ, “Itsre nɔ kɛgba nɛ sɛ, wɔnɔ ɔmɛayɛ teli yɛ aba ga mɛesi.”

ref: Jesus walked on a little way. Then he knelt with his face to the ground and prayed, “My Father, if it is possible, don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
text: Gɛ aza ga vɛ otugba kɛbɔɛ, awɔlɛ adɔ klɛ ayɛkɔ amɛnɛmɛ ekumuesi klɛ ekulukpaya sɛ, “Maka, onu gɛ kɛɛ ba za tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ tɛ ɔkɔ kantsea nɛ kafla mɛ nu. Pɔ ganɛ kana gɛ mɛ ɛpɛɛ, kana gɛ wɔ ɔpɛɛ kɛlɛ ba ba kɛlɛ mɛ.”

ref: When Peter had gone out to the gate, another servant girl saw him and said to some people there, “This man was with Jesus from Nazareth.”
text: Ɔkɛlɛ ashɛ̃ vɛ ede li kedunu, ɔkɛlɛ adzɛnuvɔɛ teli aza ba mɔɛ klɛ aaɖɔ tsre banɔ nɛ gɛ be li ɔkɛlɛ nɛ asɛ, “Anɔa nɛ gɛ elia nɛ tsyɛ aza Yesu Nazarɛtɛ eyi nɛe de. ”

ref: Then they spit in his face and hit him with their fists. Others slapped him
text: Ɔkɛlɛ be bu bututu akpɛ yɛamɛ klɛ be hu yɛ ekpli. Be teli ba bɔ yɛ dɔ

ref: But in front of everyone Peter said, “That isn't so! I don't know what you are talking about!”
text: Pɔ nɛ balɛ amɛ Petro adɛ dɛmɛ sɛ yɛ tsyɛ alɛ Yesue de. Ɔkɛlɛ aaɖɔ sɛ, “Itinyi kɛdɔnɔ nɛ gɛ nɔɔ ɖɔ.”

ref: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
text: Nɛ kɛlɛe de bɔɛ, banɔ nɛ gɛ be li kititi nɛ ɔkɛlɛ nɛ ba tɔnɔ Petro avɛ klɛ ba ɖɔ asɛ, “Tutuutu wɔ tsyɛ balɛ ɔmayɛ teli onu, alasɛ wɔ kaɖɔ ɖuɖɔ nɛ kebu wɔ akasɛ.”

ref: They made a crown out of thorn branches and placed it on his head, and they put a stick in his right hand. The soldiers knelt down and pretended to worship him. They made fun of him and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”
text: klɛ ba bhɛtɛ ewikutu klɛ ba yɛkɔ ekumu akɔɛ. Ba yɛkɔ ovusi akpɛ yɛ kɛŋahwɛ nɛm klɛ be zhi akpla nɛ yɛ otugba klɛ ba kpɛ yɛ butsɔ asɛ, “Obuii, Yuɖanɛ ekusi nɛ naa!”

ref: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
text: Gɛ ba kplaɛasɔ beke, ba tsyuɛ yɛ bawi na kpɛ aglɔm ganɔ kebisi mɛ.

ref: They left their graves, and after Jesus had risen to life, they went into the holy city, where they were seen by many people.
text: Be koeyi denɔ idzrem, klɛ nɛ Yesu tɛlulɔ nɛe de, be tsoku ɔmagbla nɛ gɛ ɔlɛ shu obhui nɛ mɛ, klɛ ba wɛ̃ nɛ banɔ gelee amɛ.

ref: John also told the people, “Someone more powerful is going to come. And I am not good enough even to stoop down and untie his sandals.
text: Klɛ yɛ kibui nɛ gɛ evunɔ kɛlɛ dzi sɛ, “Mɛe de nɛɛ anɔ teli abaa ba, gɛ alɛ kemi adɛ mɛ, anyɛa lɛlɛ nɛ afukpaohui tsyɛ ɛtɛ ba naa zhikpla kɔ ɔlɛ tɛbublue.

ref: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.
text: Ba vɛ Kapenaumɛ, klɛ gɛ Yuɖanɛ Kimuenewi nɛ na, Yesu ede tsoku oxogokɔvu nɛ mɛ klɛ akpa kɛdɔ kasɛ.

ref: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
text: Kɛbe lɛlɛ nɛ mɛ gɛ, Yesu ede Nazarɛtɛ nɛ Galelia nɛ aba, klɛ aba yɛkɛ bunikpɛkpɛ denɔ Yohanɛsɛ ɔkɔ nɛ Yɔɖan Keni nɛ mɛ.

ref: Then Jesus went to their synagogues everywhere in Galilee, where he preached and forced out demons. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
text: So anyɔ Galelia petee, aɖɔ Kpayabui nɛ oxogokɔevu nɛ mɛ klɛ akɛka ɛfufɔɛkpã. Yesu Ebeke Anyɛ Teli Bunyadzɛdzã

ref: It was during the time of Abiathar the high priest. David went into the house of God and ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are allowed to eat. He also gave some to his followers.”
text: Nɛ Abiata kpayaɔzakɔanɔ gbla nɛ kibe, Daviti etsoku Kpayavu nɛ mɛ ada tã gbɛgbɛvu gɛ be tsyina ahwɛ nɛ lɛ shu, gɛ kpayaɔzakɔanɔ nɛ ko ɔshã kɔɛ dzo sɛ ba tã. Klɛ akɔ yɛeshopoa lɛ nɛ tsyɛ teli la tã.”

ref: No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: “Anɔnyanɔ atɛ yɛkɔ kɛpɔtɛvuvɔ akamɛ atsa nɛ kɛkuku. Onu gɛ abhɛtɛ ana, kɛpɔtɛvuvɔ nɛ ba nunɔ kɛkuku klɛ kɛba tsyuɛ̃ dɛa na.

ref: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
text: Yesu etsre anyɛ ɔhwɛsisiku nɛ asɛ, “Lɔ kititi nɛ anɔnyanɔ amɛ.”

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
text: Klɛ ɛshãdɔkasɛ nɛ gɛ be zhi de Yerusalɛm aba naa ɖɔ asɛ, “Bɛlzebul gɛ enu Ɛfufɔɛkpãekusi ɔlɛ otsri yɛa bha. Ganɔ ɛfufɔɛkpã ekusieyi nɛ kabha yɛ aakɛka ɛfufɔɛkpã.”

ref: And a family that fights won't last long either.
text: Onu gɛ bupa tuli bu pɔ nɛ aglɔ shu, bupa lɛlɛ nɛ tɛ ba naa za.

ref: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
text: Kɛgbɛagba mɛ tsre nɔ, ebuizizi anɛ ebusobui nɛ petee gɛ banɔ abhɛtɛ nɛɛ ba ba yɛkɔ etsye alɛ.

ref: But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.
text: Pɔ gɛ igu dɛ za alɛ so la dɛ za nɛ ɛkpã dzidze. Kibe lɛ gɛ butɔ ɔdɔgɔsɛ tɛkpɛkpatɔnɔ ba nɛ kibui nɛ shu ko ba wɔlɛ kplitaa.

ref: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
text: Flogo pɛ eeshe edzi osi nɛ ɛshɛgbla dɛ nɛ buka nɛ mɛ, gɛ buzhuɔɛ bu baa dɔ nɛ ɔlɛ mɛ.”

ref: Jesus went with Jairus. Many people followed along and kept crowding around.
text: So Yesu atɔ nɔ anyɛ nɛ avɛ. Banɔɛza nɛ zɛ tɔnɔ yɛ, klɛ ba kana yɛ atsya.

ref: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
text: Ɔkɛlɛ adzɛ nɛ gɛ enyi kɛdɔnɔ nɛ gɛ ba yɛa bha so nɛɛ, ekoeyi aba klɛ ebe zhiadɔ nɛ yɛ otugba, klɛ eshuku nɛ kifu, klɛ etsre yɛ kɛ gɛ ba nɛ yɛa bha.

ref: The next Sabbath he taught in the synagogue. Many of the people who heard him were amazed and asked, “How can he do all this? Where did he get such wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
text: Gɛ Kimuenewi nɛ kɛ na, Yesu ada kpa kɛdɔ kasɛ nɛ oxogokɔvu nɛ mɛ. Banɔ gelee gɛ be nu yɛ kɛdɔ, kiyinɔ balɛ. “Fakɔ anɔa nɛ edenɔ ebuia nɛ gɛ aɖɔ?” “Kide gɛ nu kibui nyinyi nɛ, gɛ ba kɔ yɛa na, gɛ kɔ sɛ abhɛtɛ ebui gɛ eyinɔanɔ tsyɛ!

ref: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
text: Kɛlɛso, be ku keniabha opupu toli balɛ nahwɛ klɛ ba shɛ avɛ ede ku ɔkranakɔ toli.

ref: They sat down in groups of
text: Klɛ so ba za kesi nɛ kɛfɔɛfɔ etsum anɛ afeti etsu mɛ.

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
text: Banɔ nɛ petee ba ŋa klɛ be xui,

ref: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
text: Gɛ owulago nɛ ɔna, opupu nɔ za keni nɛ kishitɛbha, klɛ Yesu terikpo agɔ nɛ ogbigbe nɛa bha.
